Notice Of Motion And Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Jun-16-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dolores Bastian Dalton, of Goldfarb Lipman LLP, Tel. 510.836.6336, for defendants;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF JONES MEMORIAL UNITED METHODIST CHURCH'S Motion For New Trial is denied. The Court lacks jurisdiction to rule on this motion. CCP sec. 660 provides that "the power of the court to rule on a motion for a new trial shall expire 60 days from and after...service by any party of written notice of the entry of the judgment...If such a motion is not determined within said period of 60 days...the effect shall be a denial of the motion without further order of the court." The 60 day time limit is jurisdictional. (Fischer v. First Internat. Bank (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1450-51.) Defendants served a notice of entry of judgment on Plaintiff on April 10, 2015. Accordingly, the Court's power to rule on this motion expired on June 9, 2015. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Evidence
Off Calendar Feb-20-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. T. SCOTT TATE of Schnader, Harrison, Segal & Lewis, LLP, Tel No. (415) 364-6719 appearing for Plaintiff Jones Memorial United Methodist Church;
Attys. DOLORES BASTIAN DALTON and CELIA W. LEE of Goldfarb & Lipman, LLP, Tel No. (510) 836-6336 appearing for Defendants Jones Memorial Homes Inc., Jones Senior Homes, Inc., Gerald Jones, Emmett Cobb, Charles Harris, Rozelle Lee, James Mccary, Marvin Norman, and Derek Toliver (erroneously sued herein as Derek Tolliver).

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC., JONES SENIOR HOMES, INC., GERALD JONES, EMMETT COBB, CHARLES HARRIS, ROZELLE LEE, JAMES MCCARY, MARVIN NORMAN, AND DEREK TOLIVER (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS DERIK TOLLIVER)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The undisputed evidence indicates that defendant Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. is an entity independent from plaintiff and Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. amended its by-laws to become a non-member entity. Defendants correctly cite the HUD regulatory agreement that required HUD approval only for changes to the by-laws "with reference to the purposes of the corporation or the disposition of the assets upon dissolution." HUD did not need to approve membership changes. The change in the by-laws does not conflict with Jones Memorial Homes, Inc.'s Articles of Incorporation. See Health Maintenance Network v. Blue Cross (1988) 202 Cal.App.3d 1043, 1059. In addition, any conceivable statute of limitations bars plaintiff's claims. The declaration of Erma Cobb demonstrates that plaintiff was on inquiry notice regarding the issues raised in plaintiff's First Amended Complaint in March of 2007. Plaintiff filed this untimely action on March 21, 2014. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
